title: Portfolio Optimization for Commodity ETFs under Heavy-Tailed Returns
abstract: arXiv:2606.26625v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: This paper examines portfolio optimization for commodity exchange-traded funds (ETFs) under heavy-tailed return behavior. Using daily Bloomberg data for 30 U.S.-listed commodity ETFs from 12 December 2018 to 16 December 2024, we study funds spanning agriculture, energy, metals, and broad commodity index exposure. We compare a passive buy-and-hold portfolio with rolling-window optimized portfolios formed under mean--variance and conditional value-at-risk (CVaR) criteria, considering both long-only and restricted long--short strategies. The results showed substantial heterogeneity across commodity sectors, with energy and broad commodity index funds displaying pronounced volatility, skewness, and excess kurtosis. Historical optimization indicated that minimum-risk and CVaR-based portfolios provided more stable cumulative performance than tangent portfolios and generally improved Sharpe, Calmar, and STARR$_{0.95}$ ratios. translated_title: 重尾收益下的大宗商品ETF投资组合优化
translated_abstract: 本文研究了在重尾收益行为下的大宗商品交易所交易基金（ETF）的投资组合优化问题。利用2018年12月12日至2024年12月16日期间30只美国上市大宗商品ETF的每日彭博数据，我们研究了涵盖农业、能源、金属及广泛大宗商品指数敞口的基金。我们将被动买入持有策略与在均值-方差和条件风险价值（CVaR）准则下形成的滚动窗口优化投资组合进行了比较，考虑了仅做多和限制性多空两种策略。结果显示，不同大宗商品板块之间存在显著异质性，其中能源和广泛大宗商品指数基金表现出明显的波动性、偏度和超额峰度。历史优化表明，基于最小风险和CVaR的投资组合相比切线投资组合提供了更稳定的累计表现，并普遍提高了夏普比率、卡尔玛比率和STARR$_{0.95}$比率。极端值诊断...
tldr: 本文通过实证发现，在重尾收益特征下，基于最小风险和条件风险价值（CVaR）准则优化的大宗商品ETF投资组合，相比传统策略能提供更稳定的累计表现并显著提升风险调整后收益指标。
en_tdlr: This paper empirically finds that under heavy-tailed returns, commodity ETF portfolios optimized via minimum-risk and conditional value-at-risk (CVaR) criteria provide more stable cumulative performance and significantly improve risk-adjusted return metrics compared to traditional strategies.
